subject: [PATCH 07/11] mm/zsmalloc, zswap: Handle objcg charging and lifetime in zsmalloc

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the obj_cgroup_get() reference is for the pointer, not the charge, and suggested moving it to when adding and storing zspage->objcgs[], to avoid confusion in verifying the series.

The reviewer noted that the patch was misleadingly acquiring a reference to entry->objcg before checking if objcg is NULL, and suggested reordering the code to avoid this issue.
reviews.raw_body:
Note that the obj_cgroup_get() reference is for the pointer, not the
charge. I think it all comes out right in the end, but it's a bit
confusing to follow and verify through the series.

IOW, it's better move that obj_cgroup_get() when you add and store
zspage->objcgs[]. If zswap stil has a reference at that point in the
series, then it's fine for there to be two separate obj_cgroup_get()
as well, with later patches deleting the zswap one when its
entry->objcg pointer disappears.

---

[ I can see that this was misleading. It was really getting a
  reference for the entry->objcg = objcg a few lines down, hitching a
  ride on that existing `if (objcg)`. ]

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged a concern about the placement of code and promised to make it more explicit in a future version.

Author agrees to restructure code in zsmalloc.c to make it clear that objcg charging and lifetime management happens before setting the objcg pointer, and also moves freeing section after setting pointer to NULL.

Author acknowledged that Johannes Weiner's feedback about obj_cgroup_charge_zswap() and obj_cgroup_uncharge_zswap() being removed was correct, but did not address the issue or provide a plan for re-adding these functions.
reviews.raw_body:
Thank you for pointing that out. I'll try to make it more explicit via
the placement.

---

Sounds good with me. Maybe for the code block above I just move it one
line up so that it happens before the zspage->objcgs set and
make it more obvious that it's associated with setting the objcg
pointer and not with the charge?

And for the freeing section, putting after we set the pointer to
NULL could be more obvious?

---

Thank you for the clarification! I hope you have a great day : -)
Joshua

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: The reviewer responded positively, stating that the patch 'makes sense' and expressing gratitude.
reviews.raw_body:
That makes sense to me!

Thanks

2026-03-11.patch_summary: This patch updates the zsmalloc layer to handle object cgroup (objcg) charging and lifetime management, specifically for zswap entries. It changes how memory is accounted for in zswap, no longer tracking compressed object size but instead the size of the slot occupied by the entry. This brings memory consumption representation closer to accuracy. Additionally, it removes an unnecessary newline in obj_free and updates related functions.
